purpose: Machine-readable validation-lane plan for FrankenLibC. Maps each common surface (harness tests, runtime_math, ABI subsystems, fuzz build smokes, bench build smokes, standalone forge gates, workload replays) to the minimal `rch` command and CARGO_TARGET_DIR isolation rule that an agent in a 64+ core swarm must use. Reduces overlap (two agents on the same target dir thrash incremental compilation) and prevents agents from running unscoped workspace gates when a focused crate gate would suffice.

rules.rationale_no_local_cargo: AGENTS.md requires `rch cargo ...` for any CPU-intensive cargo invocation. Local cargo on this 64-core node would burn the swarm's shared cache and block peer agents.
rules.local_fallback_is_invalid_proof: `[RCH] local (remote execution failed)` is invalid validation evidence for this repo. Stop that wrapper and rerun with remote-only force instead of treating local fallback as a pass.
rules.target_dir_isolation_rationale: Per-bead, per-agent CARGO_TARGET_DIR avoids incremental-compilation contention when two agents touch the same crate at the same time. The bead-short suffix is the last segment of the bead id (e.g. juvqm11 for bd-juvqm.11).
rules.broad_workspace_gate_policy: FORBIDDEN unless the bead explicitly touches workspace-wide infrastructure (Cargo.toml at the workspace root, profile changes, or a cross-crate trait). Default to focused `-p <crate>` gates.
rules.post_remote_exit_hang_policy: When `rch cargo ...` reports remote `exit=0` but artifact retrieval stalls past 5 minutes, do NOT re-run. Verify the work landed via `git log --oneline -3 -- <changed-files>` and `br show <bead>`. Re-running burns tape on a peer's already-completed retrieval and corrupts the shared target dir.
rules.fmt_policy: `cargo fmt --check` is fast-local-only; never wrap fmt in rch.
rules.clippy_lane: `rch cargo clippy -p <crate> --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings` is the default focused lane. `--all-targets` only when the bead explicitly touches integration tests or examples.

must_reject: workspace-wide cargo gates inside focused surface lanes
cargo lanes without a package selector
cargo lanes without target directory isolation guidance
bash-wrapped cargo lanes that rch cannot classify
[RCH] local fallback treated as valid evidence
broad gate allowlist rows without a narrow rationale

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --lib tail_stats
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --tests --no-default-features -- -D warnings
surfaces.broad_gate_forbidden_reason: Each conformance test is independent; running --tests under --all-targets recompiles 100+ test binaries per invocation.

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib runtime_math
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib pthread_abi
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib stdio_abi
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib string_abi
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --lib stdlib
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --lib resolv
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --lib --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.scope: frankenlibc-fuzz fuzz targets (one bin at a time)
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-fuzz --bin <target_name> -- -D warnings
surfaces.broad_gate_forbidden_reason: frankenlibc-fuzz has 60+ bin targets; checking all of them per fuzz-target edit wastes 10+ minutes per agent.

surfaces.scope: frankenlibc-bench harness=false bins (one bench at a time)
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-bench --benches --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test user_workload_replay_<variant>
surfaces.minimal_clippy_cmd: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --tests --no-default-features -- -D warnings

surfaces.minimal_test_cmd: scripts/check_<gate>.sh (rch is invoked by the script itself; agent must NOT prefix with `rch cargo ...`)
surfaces.target_dir_pattern: set inside the script via the project's standard CARGO_TARGET_DIR helper

broad_gate_allowlist.rationale: Permitted only when a Cargo.toml at the workspace root or a profile section changes. Bead must cite the workspace-touching file.
Permitted only for release-readiness sanity checks; the bead must cite the release tag or pre-merge audit it satisfies.

post_remote_exit_hang_runbook: 2. `git log --oneline -3 -- <files-the-bead-was-supposed-to-touch>` - if the commit landed, the work is done.
3. `br show <bead>` - if status is closed, the work is done.
4. Preserve the visible `rch` remote exit=0 line or worker-side PASS summary as validation evidence.
5. `ls /data/tmp/claude-1000/<session>/tasks/<task-id>.output` - if non-zero size and ends with `[RCH] remote ts2 (Ns)`, retrieval is in flight; wait additional 60s before any remediation.
6. Only after steps 2-5 confirm the work did NOT land, consider re-running with a different CARGO_TARGET_DIR to bypass a corrupted lock.
regeneration_note: Add a new entry under `surfaces` whenever a bead introduces a new crate, top-level module, or test file pattern that other agents will routinely validate. Do not delete entries; deprecate them via a `deprecated_at_commit` field instead so peers running off older checkouts can still resolve their lane.
